article_title: Nvidia Takes a $2.1 Billion Warrant in IREN and Signs a $3.4 Billion Five-Year Cloud Contract, Anchoring a Joint 5 GW AI Buildout

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: consent.yahoo.com

content_preview.title: Nvidia Takes a $2.1 Billion Warrant in IREN and Signs a $3.4 Billion Five-Year Cloud Contract, Anchoring a Joint 5 GW AI Buildout
content_preview.summary: Nvidia receives a five-year option on 30 million IREN shares at $70 each while paying IREN $3.4 billion over five years for Blackwell cloud capacity at Childress, Texas.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Nvidia and IREN on May 7 announced a strategic partnership covering up to 5 gigawatts of NVIDIA DSX-aligned AI infrastructure across IREN's global data-center footprint, paired with a five-year warrant that gives Nvidia the right to acquire up to 30 million IREN ordinary shares at $70 each — an investment right of up to $2.1 billion subject to regulatory conditions, [according to Nvidia's announcement](https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/nvidia-and-iren-announce-strategic-partnership...

editor_notes.content_quality: Substantive 1,111-word Analysis-grade News piece (above the News range — sits comfortably as Analysis). Strong structure: separation of partnership terms, cloud contract, market reaction, unknowns, and analysis. Cross-reference to the IREN-Mirantis acquisition piece is genuinely load-bearing rather than decorative.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 3 snapshots from disk. NVIDIA news (source-0): $2.1B warrant, 30 million shares at $70, 5 gigawatts, Sweetwater 2-gigawatt Texas campus, NVIDIA DSX architecture, Jensen Huang quote 'AI factories are becoming foundational infrastructure for the global economy. Deploying these systems at scale requires deep integration across the full stack' verbatim, Daniel Roberts attribution, May 7 announcement — all verified verbatim. GlobeNewswire IREN release (source-1): $3.4 billion five-year contract, air-cooled Blackwell platform systems, approximately 60MW of IREN's existing data centers, Childress Texas campus, Mirantis as orchestration layer, Daniel Roberts 'fully managed cloud solutions, not just bare metal' quote verbatim. Yahoo Finance/Motley Fool (source-2) redirected to consent.yahoo.com — 9% stock reaction figure attributed to Motley Fool is unverifiable from snapshot, but it sits in a non-core 'Market reaction' section and not in the lead. Cross-referenced article (2026-05/06-iren-acquires-mirantis-...) verified to exist in the archive.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every core specific (warrant terms, share count, exercise price, contract value, MW capacity, Blackwell, Childress location, Mirantis orchestration) traces to NVIDIA news or GlobeNewswire IREN release.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral; analysis section is appropriately framed as analysis rather than reporting.
editor_notes.originality: No archive collision. Genuine follow-up to the May 6 IREN-Mirantis acquisition article — the cross-reference is editorially load-bearing because the Mirantis software stack appears in the cloud contract.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE. Multi-sourced, well-structured, core facts verified verbatim from NVIDIA + GlobeNewswire. The 9% market-reaction figure attributed to Motley Fool is unverifiable due to Yahoo consent gate but sits in a non-core position.
